product_updates.title: Sam Altman’s project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
product_updates.content: World, which has raised eyebrows (but also a lot of interest) with its Orb-centered anonymous verification project, is looking to expand its influence via a bevy of new partnerships.

product_updates.title: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles exit OpenAI as company continues to shed ‘side quests’
product_updates.content: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles are leaving OpenAI as the company shuts down Sora and folds its science team, signaling a sharp pivot away from consumer moonshots toward enterprise AI.

product_updates.title: Sources: Cursor in talks to raise $2B+ at $50B valuation as enterprise growth surges
product_updates.content: Returning backers a16z and Thrive are expected to lead the round.

product_updates.title: ‘Tokenmaxxing’ is making developers less productive than they think
product_updates.content: There's a lot more code — but it's a lot more expensive and requires a lot more rewriting.

product_updates.title: Tokenmaxxing, OpenAI’s shopping spree, and the AI Anxiety Gap
product_updates.content: The gap between AI insiders and everyone else is widening, and the spending, suspicion, and even new vocabulary are starting to show it. While OpenAI is busy buying up everything from finance apps to talk shows, a certain shoe company just rebranded as an AI infrastructure play, and Anthropic unv…

product_updates.title: Anthropic launches Claude Design, a new product for creating quick visuals
product_updates.content: The company says Claude Design is intended to help people like founders and product managers without a design background share their ideas more easily.

technical_breakthroughs.title: US-sanctioned currency exchange says $15 million heist done by "unfriendly states"
technical_breakthroughs.content: Grinex says needed hacking resources "available exclusively to ... unfriendly states."

technical_breakthroughs.title: How robots learn: A brief, contemporary history
technical_breakthroughs.content: Roboticists used to dream big but build small. They’d hope to match or exceed the extraordinary complexity of the human body, and then they’d spend their career refining robotic arms for auto plants. Aim for C-3P0; end up with the Roomba. The real ambition for many of these researchers was the ro…
